旧[[県社]] |創建 = （伝）[[飛鳥時代]] |本殿 = 一間社[[春日造]]桧皮葺 |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[10月26日]] |神事 = |地図 = Japan Nara }} [[File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''都祁水分神社'''（つげみくまりじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]都祁友田町にある[[神社]]。 == 祭神 == 祭神は次の3柱。 * [[ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|速秋津彦神]] * [[水分神|天水分神]]（あまのみくまりのかみ/あめのみくまりのかみ） * [[水分神|国水分神]]（くにのみくまりのかみ） == 歴史 == === 概史 === 創建は不詳だが、社伝によると[[飛鳥時代]]の創建とされる。当初は[[都祁山口神社 (奈良市)|都祁山口神社]]（小山戸）の地に鎮座していたとされ、現在の鎮座地は[[聖武天皇]]が行幸の際に使用された堀越頓宮の伝承地であり、[[平安時代]]には都介頓宮となり、伊勢[[斎宮]]の皇女の宿場として使用されたという。 [[延喜式]]には大社に列し、[[月次祭|月次]]、[[新嘗祭|新嘗]]の官幣に預かっている。 [[中世]]には社領は[[興福寺]]喜多院二階堂の[[荘園]]となり、開発が進むと共に水分神は信仰を集め、[[天禄]]3年（[[972年]]）[[9月25日]]、荘園の中央である友田（現社地）へ遷座した。[[鎌倉時代]]初頭には喜多院二階堂領は同じ興福寺の大乗院領に変わり、[[明応]]8年（[[1499年]]）には現在の本殿が建立された。 === 神階 === * [[承和 (日本)|承和]]7年（[[840年]]）[[10月7日]]、無位から従五位下 （『[[続日本後紀]]』）- 表記は「水分神」 * [[天安 (日本)|天安]]3年（[[859年]]）[[1月27日]]、従五位下から正五位下 （『[[日本三代実録]]』）- 表記は「都祁水分神」 == 境内 == ファイル:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Maiden.jpg|舞殿 ファイル:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Haiden.jpg|拝殿 ファイル:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿（国指定重要文化財） ファイル:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Chokushaden.jpg|直舎殿 == 脚注 == == 参考文献 == * 社頭案内板 == 関連項目 == * [[水分神]] * [[大和国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.tsugemikumari.or.jp 公式サイト] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=奈良県}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つけみくまりしんしや}} [[Category:奈良市の神社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大つけみくまりしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:式内大社]]

旧[[県社]] |創建 = （伝）[[飛鳥時代]] |本殿 = 一間社[[春日造]]桧皮葺 |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[10月26日]] |神事 = |地図 = Japan Nara }} [[File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''都祁水分神社'''（つげみくまりじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]都祁友田町にある[[神社]]。[[式内大社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]。 == 祭神 == 祭神は次の3柱。 * [[ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|速秋津彦神]] * [[水分神|天水分神]]（あまのみくまりのかみ/あめのみくまりのかみ） * [[水分神|国水分神]]（くにのみくまりのかみ） == 歴史 == === 概史 === 創建は不詳だが、社伝によると[[飛鳥時代]]の創建とされる。当初は[[都祁山口神社 (奈良市)|都祁山口神社]]（小山戸）の地に鎮座していたとされ、現在の鎮座地は[[聖武天皇]]が行幸の際に使用された堀越頓宮の伝承地であり、[[平安時代]]には都介頓宮となり、伊勢[[斎宮]]の皇女の宿場として使用されたという。 [[延喜式]]には大社に列し、[[月次祭|月次]]、[[新嘗祭|新嘗]]の官幣に預かっている。 [[中世]]には社領は[[興福寺]]喜多院二階堂の[[荘園]]となり、開発が進むと共に水分神は信仰を集め、[[天禄]]3年（[[972年]]）[[9月25日]]、荘園の中央である友田（現社地）へ遷座した。[[鎌倉時代]]初頭には喜多院二階堂領は同じ興福寺の大乗院領に変わり、[[明応]]8年（[[1499年]]）には現在の本殿が建立された。 === 神階 === * [[承和 (日本)|承和]]7年（[[840年]]）[[10月7日]]、無位から従五位下 （『[[続日本後紀]]』）- 表記は「水分神」 * [[天安 (日本)|天安]]3年（[[859年]]）[[1月27日]]、従五位下から正五位下 （『[[日本三代実録]]』）- 表記は「都祁水分神」 == 境内 == ファイル:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Maiden.jpg|舞殿 ファイル:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Haiden.jpg|拝殿 ファイル:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿（国の[[重要文化財]]） ファイル:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Chokushaden.jpg|直会殿 == 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * 都祁水分神社本殿（附 棟札6枚）（建造物） - 室町時代後期、明応8年（1499年）の造営。1907年（明治40年）8月28日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|102|2676|都祁水分神社本殿}}。 === 奈良県指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 金銅装神輿（工芸品） - 室町時代、康正3年（1456年）の作。1975年（昭和50年）3月31日指定{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf 奈良県指定文化財一覧]}}（奈良県ホームページ）。。 == 脚注 == == 参考文献 == * 社頭案内板 == 関連項目 == * [[水分神]] * [[大和国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Tsuge-mikumari-jinja}} * [http://www.tsugemikumari.or.jp 都祁水分神社] - 公式サイト * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=20501 都祁水分神社] - 國學院大學21世紀COEプログラム「神道・神社史料集成」 {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=奈良県}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つけみくまりしんしや}} [[Category:奈良市の神社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大つけみくまりしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:奈良県の重要文化財]]

旧[[県社]] |創建 = （伝）[[飛鳥時代]] |本殿 = 一間社[[春日造]]桧皮葺 |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[10月26日]] |神事 = |地図 = Japan Nara }} [[File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''都祁水分神社'''（つげみくまりじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]都祁友田町にある[[神社]]。[[式内大社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]。 == 祭神 == 祭神は次の3柱。 * [[ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|速秋津彦神]] * [[水分神|天水分神]]（あまのみくまりのかみ/あめのみくまりのかみ） * [[水分神|国水分神]]（くにのみくまりのかみ） == 歴史 == === 概史 === 創建は不詳だが、社伝によると[[飛鳥時代]]の創建とされる。当初は[[都祁山口神社 (奈良市)|都祁山口神社]]（小山戸）の地に鎮座していたとされ、現在の鎮座地は[[聖武天皇]]が行幸の際に使用された堀越頓宮の伝承地であり、[[平安時代]]には都介頓宮となり、伊勢[[斎宮]]の皇女の宿場として使用されたという。 [[延喜式]]には大社に列し、[[月次祭|月次]]、[[新嘗祭|新嘗]]の官幣に預かっている。 [[中世]]には社領は[[興福寺]]喜多院二階堂の[[荘園]]となり、開発が進むと共に水分神は信仰を集め、[[天禄]]3年（[[972年]]）[[9月25日]]、荘園の中央である友田（現社地）へ遷座した。[[鎌倉時代]]初頭には喜多院二階堂領は同じ興福寺の大乗院領に変わり、[[明応]]8年（[[1499年]]）には現在の本殿が建立された。 === 神階 === * [[承和 (日本)|承和]]7年（[[840年]]）[[10月7日]]、無位から従五位下 （『[[続日本後紀]]』）- 表記は「水分神」 * [[天安 (日本)|天安]]3年（[[859年]]）[[1月27日]]、従五位下から正五位下 （『[[日本三代実録]]』）- 表記は「都祁水分神」 == 境内 == ファイル:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Maiden.jpg|舞殿 ファイル:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Haiden.jpg|拝殿 ファイル:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿（国の[[重要文化財]]） ファイル:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Chokushaden.jpg|直会殿 == 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * 都祁水分神社本殿（附 棟札6枚）（建造物） - 室町時代後期、明応8年（1499年）の造営。1907年（明治40年）8月28日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|102|2676|都祁水分神社本殿}}。 === 奈良県指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 金銅装神輿（工芸品） - 室町時代、康正3年（1456年）の作。1975年（昭和50年）3月31日指定{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf 奈良県指定文化財一覧]}}（奈良県ホームページ）。。 == 脚注 == == 参考文献 == * 社頭案内板 == 関連項目 == * [[水分神]] * [[闘鶏国造]] * [[大和国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Tsuge-mikumari-jinja}} * [http://www.tsugemikumari.or.jp 都祁水分神社] - 公式サイト * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=20501 都祁水分神社] - 國學院大學21世紀COEプログラム「神道・神社史料集成」 {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=奈良県}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つけみくまりしんしや}} [[Category:奈良市の神社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大つけみくまりしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:奈良県の重要文化財]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]]

revision.text: #redirect[[en:Tsuge Mikumari Shrine]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

'''Tsuge Mikumari Shrine''' (都祁水分神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|神社}} located in {{ill|Nara City|en|奈良市}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|en|式内社}} (major shrine) and formerly held the rank of a [[prefectural shrine]] under the old shrine ranking system.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following three deities: * {{ill|Hayaki-hiko and Hayaki-hime|en|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|en|天水分神}} (Heavenly Water Distribution Deity) * {{ill|Kuni-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|en|国水分神}} (Earthly Water Distribution Deity)
== History == ### Early History The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is traditionally believed to have been established during the {{ill|Asuka period|en|飛鳥時代}}. Initially located at the site of {{ill|Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine|ja|都祁山口神社}}, it was later moved to its current location, believed to have been used as the Horigoe Temporary Palace during the reign of Emperor Shōmu. The area served as a lodging site for the imperial princesses who were priestesses of the Ise Grand Shrine during the {{ill|Heian period|en|平安時代}}.
The shrine was listed as a major shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|延喜式}} and received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami Festival|en|月次祭}} and {{ill|Niiname Festival|en|新嘗祭}}.
### Medieval Period During the medieval period, the shrine's lands became part of the estate of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|en|興福寺}}, specifically under the Kita-in sub-temple of the Nikaidō lineage. As the estate developed, the worship of water deities grew in importance. In 972 (Tenroku 3), the shrine was relocated to its current site in Tomoda. The current main shrine building was constructed in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the Muromachi period.
### Divine Ranks * {{ill|Shōwa (era)|en|承和}} 7 (840), October 7: Elevated from unranked to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower ({{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|続日本後紀}}) * {{ill|Tian’an|en|天安}} 3 (859), January 27: Elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|日本三代実録}})
== Grounds == <gallery> File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Maiden.jpg|舞殿 (Dance Hall) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Haiden.jpg|拝殿 (Worship Hall) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 (Main Shrine, Important Cultural Property) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Chokushaden.jpg|直会殿 (Banquet Hall) </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == ### Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) * **Main Shrine** (附 棟札6枚) - Built in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the late Muromachi period. Designated on August 28, 1907.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|102|2676|都祁水分神社本殿}}</ref>
### Nara Prefectural Designation * **Tangible Cultural Property** * **Gilded Bronze Mikoshi** (Portable Shrine) - Constructed in 1456 (Kōshō 3). Designated on March 31, 1975.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Official Website).</ref>
== See Also == * {{ill|Mikumari deity|en|水分神}} * {{ill|Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko|en|闘鶏国造}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|en|大和国の式内社一覧}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuge-mikumari-jinja}} * [http://www.tsugemikumari.or.jp Official Website of Tsuge Mikumari Shrine] * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=20501 Tsuge Mikumari Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program: Shinto and Shrine Historical Resources Collection
{{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha shrines]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Nara City]][[category:new pages]] [[ja:都祁水分神社]]

revision.text:
'''Tsuge Mikumari Shrine''' (都祁水分神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|神社}} located in {{ill|Nara City|en|奈良市}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|en|式内社}} (major shrine) and formerly held the rank of a [[prefectural shrine]] under the old shrine ranking system.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following three deities: * {{ill|Hayaki-hiko and Hayaki-hime|en|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|en|天水分神}} (Heavenly Water Distribution Deity) * {{ill|Kuni-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|en|国水分神}} (Earthly Water Distribution Deity)
== History == ### Early History The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is traditionally believed to have been established during the {{ill|Asuka period|en|飛鳥時代}}. Initially located at the site of {{ill|Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine|ja|都祁山口神社}}, it was later moved to its current location, believed to have been used as the Horigoe Temporary Palace during the reign of Emperor Shōmu. The area served as a lodging site for the imperial princesses who were priestesses of the Ise Grand Shrine during the {{ill|Heian period|en|平安時代}}.
The shrine was listed as a major shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|延喜式}} and received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami Festival|en|月次祭}} and {{ill|Niiname Festival|en|新嘗祭}}.
### Medieval Period During the medieval period, the shrine's lands became part of the estate of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|en|興福寺}}, specifically under the Kita-in sub-temple of the Nikaidō lineage. As the estate developed, the worship of water deities grew in importance. In 972 (Tenroku 3), the shrine was relocated to its current site in Tomoda. The current main shrine building was constructed in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the Muromachi period.
### Divine Ranks * {{ill|Shōwa (era)|en|承和}} 7 (840), October 7: Elevated from unranked to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower ({{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|続日本後紀}}) * {{ill|Tian’an|en|天安}} 3 (859), January 27: Elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|日本三代実録}})
== Grounds == <gallery> File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Maiden.jpg|舞殿 (Dance Hall) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Haiden.jpg|拝殿 (Worship Hall) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 (Main Shrine, Important Cultural Property) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Chokushaden.jpg|直会殿 (Banquet Hall) </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == ### Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) * '''Main Shrine''' (附 棟札6枚) - Built in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the late Muromachi period. Designated on August 28, 1907.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|102|2676|都祁水分神社本殿}}</ref>
### Nara Prefectural Designation * '''Tangible Cultural Property''' * '''Gilded Bronze Mikoshi''' (Portable Shrine) - Constructed in 1456 (Kōshō 3). Designated on March 31, 1975.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Official Website).</ref>
== See Also == * {{ill|Mikumari deity|en|水分神}} * {{ill|Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko|en|闘鶏国造}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|en|大和国の式内社一覧}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuge-mikumari-jinja}} * [http://www.tsugemikumari.or.jp Official Website of Tsuge Mikumari Shrine] * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=20501 Tsuge Mikumari Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program: Shinto and Shrine Historical Resources Collection
{{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha shrines]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Nara City]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[ja:都祁水分神社]]

'''Tsuge Mikumari Shrine''' (都祁水分神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (major shrine) and formerly held the rank of a [[prefectural shrine]] under the old shrine ranking system.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following three deities: * {{ill|Hayaki-hiko and Hayaki-hime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|天水分神}} (Heavenly Water Distribution Deity) * {{ill|Kuni-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|国水分神}} (Earthly Water Distribution Deity)
== History ==
=== Early History === The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is traditionally believed to have been established during the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. Initially located at the site of {{ill|Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine|ja|都祁山口神社}}, it was later moved to its current location, believed to have been used as the Horigoe Temporary Palace during the reign of Emperor Shōmu. The area served as a lodging site for the imperial princesses who were priestesses of the Ise Grand Shrine during the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}.
The shrine was listed as a major shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} and received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami Festival|ja|月次祭}} and {{ill|Niiname Festival|ja|新嘗祭}}.
=== Medieval Period === During the medieval period, the shrine's lands became part of the estate of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|ja|興福寺}}, specifically under the Kita-in sub-temple of the Nikaidō lineage. As the estate developed, the worship of water deities grew in importance. In 972 (Tenroku 3), the shrine was relocated to its current site in Tomoda. The current main shrine building was constructed in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the Muromachi period.
=== Divine Ranks === * {{ill|Shōwa (era)|ja|承和}} 7 (840), October 7: Elevated from unranked to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower ({{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本後紀}}) * {{ill|Tian’an|ja|天安}} 3 (859), January 27: Elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}})
== Grounds == <gallery> File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Maiden.jpg|舞殿 (Dance Hall) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Haiden.jpg|拝殿 (Worship Hall) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 (Main Shrine, Important Cultural Property) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Chokushaden.jpg|直会殿 (Banquet Hall) </gallery>
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * '''Main Shrine''' (附 棟札6枚) - Built in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the late Muromachi period. Designated on August 28, 1907.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|102|2676|都祁水分神社本殿}}</ref>
=== Nara Prefectural Designation === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''' * '''Gilded Bronze Mikoshi''' (Portable Shrine) - Constructed in 1456 (Kōshō 3). Designated on March 31, 1975.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Official Website).</ref>
== See Also == * {{ill|Mikumari deity|ja|水分神}} * {{ill|Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|大和国の式内社一覧}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuge-mikumari-jinja}} * [http://www.tsugemikumari.or.jp Official Website of Tsuge Mikumari Shrine] * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=20501 Tsuge Mikumari Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program: Shinto and Shrine Historical Resources Collection

'''Tsuge Mikumari Shrine''' (都祁水分神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (major shrine) and formerly held the rank of a [[prefectural shrine]] under the old shrine ranking system.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following three deities: * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ}} * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|lt=Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|天水分神}} (Heavenly Water Distribution Deity) * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|lt=Kuni-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|国水分神}} (Earthly Water Distribution Deity)
== History ==
=== Early History === The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is traditionally believed to have been established during the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. Initially located at the site of {{ill|Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine|ja|都祁山口神社}}, it was later moved to its current location, believed to have been used as the Horigoe Temporary Palace during the reign of Emperor Shōmu. The area served as a lodging site for the imperial princesses who were priestesses of the Ise Grand Shrine during the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}.
The shrine was listed as a major shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} and received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-matsuri|ja|月次祭}} and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|ja|新嘗祭}}.
=== Medieval Period === During the medieval period, the shrine's lands became part of the estate of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|ja|興福寺}}, specifically under the Kita-in sub-temple of the Nikaidō lineage. As the estate developed, the worship of water deities grew in importance. In 972 (Tenroku 3), the shrine was relocated to its current site in Tomoda. The current main shrine building was constructed in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the Muromachi period.

Designated on March 31, 1975.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Official Website).</ref>

== Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following three deities: * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ}} * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|lt=Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|天水分神}} (Heavenly Water Distribution Deity) * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|lt=Kuni-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|国水分神}} (Earthly Water Distribution Deity)
== History ==
=== Early History === The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is traditionally believed to have been established during the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. Initially located at the site of {{ill|Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine|ja|都祁山口神社}}, it was later moved to its current location, believed to have been used as the Horigoe Temporary Palace during the reign of Emperor Shōmu. The area served as a lodging site for the imperial princesses who were priestesses of the Ise Grand Shrine during the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}.
The shrine was listed as a major shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} and received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-matsuri|ja|月次祭}} and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|ja|新嘗祭}}.
=== Medieval Period === During the medieval period, the shrine's lands became part of the estate of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|ja|興福寺}}, specifically under the Kita-in sub-temple of the Nikaidō lineage. As the estate developed, the worship of water deities grew in importance. In 972 (Tenroku 3), the shrine was relocated to its current site in Tomoda. The current main shrine building was constructed in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the Muromachi period.
=== Divine Ranks === * {{ill|Shōwa (era)|ja|承和}} 7 (840), October 7: Elevated from unranked to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower ({{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本後紀}}) * {{ill|Tian’an|ja|天安}} 3 (859), January 27: Elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}})
== Grounds == <gallery> File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Maiden.jpg|舞殿 (Dance Hall) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Haiden.jpg|拝殿 (Worship Hall) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 (Main Shrine, Important Cultural Property) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Chokushaden.jpg|直会殿 (Banquet Hall) </gallery>
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * '''Main Shrine''' (附 棟札6枚) - Built in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the late Muromachi period. Designated on August 28, 1907.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|102|2676|都祁水分神社本殿}}</ref>
=== Nara Prefectural Designation === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''' * '''Gilded Bronze Mikoshi''' (Portable Shrine) - Constructed in 1456 (Kōshō 3). Designated on March 31, 1975.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Official Website).</ref>
== See Also == * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|fr}} * {{ill|Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|大和国の式内社一覧}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuge-mikumari-jinja}} * [http://www.tsugemikumari.or.jp Official Website of Tsuge Mikumari Shrine] * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=20501 Tsuge Mikumari Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program: Shinto and Shrine Historical Resources Collection
{{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha shrines]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Nara City]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsuge Mikumari Shrine}}[[ja:都祁水分神社]]

revision.text:
'''Tsuge Mikumari Shrine''' (都祁水分神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (major shrine) and formerly held the rank of a [[prefectural shrine]] under the old shrine ranking system.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following three deities: * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ}} * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|lt=Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|天水分神}} (Heavenly Water Distribution Deity) * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|lt=Kuni-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|国水分神}} (Earthly Water Distribution Deity)
== History ==
=== Early History === The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is traditionally believed to have been established during the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. Initially located at the site of {{ill|Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine|ja|都祁山口神社}}, it was later moved to its current location, believed to have been used as the Horigoe Temporary Palace during the reign of Emperor Shōmu. The area served as a lodging site for the imperial princesses who were priestesses of the Ise Grand Shrine during the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}.
The shrine was listed as a major shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} and received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-matsuri|ja|月次祭}} and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|ja|新嘗祭}}.
=== Medieval Period === During the medieval period, the shrine's lands became part of the estate of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|ja|興福寺}}, specifically under the Kita-in sub-temple of the Nikaidō lineage. As the estate developed, the worship of water deities grew in importance. In 972 (Tenroku 3), the shrine was relocated to its current site in Tomoda. The current main shrine building was constructed in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the Muromachi period.
=== Divine Ranks === * {{ill|Shōwa (era)|ja|承和}} 7 (840), October 7: Elevated from unranked to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower ({{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本後紀}}) * {{ill|Tian’an|ja|天安}} 3 (859), January 27: Elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}})
== Grounds == <gallery> File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Maiden.jpg|舞殿 (Dance Hall) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Haiden.jpg|拝殿 (Worship Hall) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 (Main Shrine, Important Cultural Property) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Chokushaden.jpg|直会殿 (Banquet Hall) </gallery>
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * '''Main Shrine''' (附 棟札6枚) - Built in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the late Muromachi period. Designated on August 28, 1907.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|102|2676|都祁水分神社本殿}}</ref>
=== Nara Prefectural Designation === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''' * '''Gilded Bronze Mikoshi''' (Portable Shrine) - Constructed in 1456 (Kōshō 3). Designated on March 31, 1975.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Official Website).</ref>
== See Also == * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|fr}} * {{ill|Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|大和国の式内社一覧}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuge-mikumari-jinja}} * [http://www.tsugemikumari.or.jp Official Website of Tsuge Mikumari Shrine] * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=20501 Tsuge Mikumari Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program: Shinto and Shrine Historical Resources Collection

'''Tsuge Mikumari Shrine''' (都祁水分神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (major shrine) and formerly held the rank of a [[prefectural shrine]] under the old shrine ranking system.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following three deities: * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ}} * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|lt=Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|天水分神}} (Heavenly Water Distribution Deity) * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|lt=Kuni-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|国水分神}} (Earthly Water Distribution Deity)
== History ==
=== Early History === The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is traditionally believed to have been established during the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. Initially located at the site of {{ill|Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine|ja|都祁山口神社}}, it was later moved to its current location, believed to have been used as the Horigoe Temporary Palace during the reign of Emperor Shōmu. The area served as a lodging site for the imperial princesses who were priestesses of the Ise Grand Shrine during the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}.
The shrine was listed as a major shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} and received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-matsuri|ja|月次祭}} and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|ja|新嘗祭}}.
=== Medieval Period === During the medieval period, the shrine's lands became part of the estate of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|ja|興福寺}}, specifically under the Kita-in sub-temple of the Nikaidō lineage. As the estate developed, the worship of water deities grew in importance. In 972 (Tenroku 3), the shrine was relocated to its current site in Tomoda. The current main shrine building was constructed in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the Muromachi period.
=== Divine Ranks === * {{ill|Shōwa (era)|ja|承和}} 7 (840), October 7: Elevated from unranked to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower ({{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本後紀}}) * {{ill|Tian’an|ja|天安}} 3 (859), January 27: Elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}})
== Grounds == <gallery> File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Maiden.jpg|舞殿 (Dance Hall) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Haiden.jpg|拝殿 (Worship Hall) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 (Main Shrine, Important Cultural Property) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Chokushaden.jpg|直会殿 (Banquet Hall) </gallery>
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * '''Main Shrine''' (附 棟札6枚) - Built in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the late Muromachi period. Designated on August 28, 1907.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|102|2676|都祁水分神社本殿}}</ref>
=== Nara Prefectural Designation === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''' * '''Gilded Bronze Mikoshi''' (Portable Shrine) - Constructed in 1456 (Kōshō 3). Designated on March 31, 1975.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Official Website).</ref>
== See Also == * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|fr}} * {{ill|Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|大和国の式内社一覧}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuge-mikumari-jinja}} * [http://www.tsugemikumari.or.jp Official Website of Tsuge Mikumari Shrine] * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=20501 Tsuge Mikumari Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program: Shinto and Shrine Historical Resources Collection

Initially located at the site of {{ill|Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine|ja|都祁山口神社}}, it was later moved to its current location, believed to have been used as the Horigoe Temporary Palace during the reign of Emperor Shōmu. The area served as a lodging site for the imperial princesses who were priestesses of the Ise Grand Shrine during the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}.
The shrine was listed as a major shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} and received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-matsuri|ja|月次祭}} and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|ja|新嘗祭}}.
=== Medieval Period === During the medieval period, the shrine's lands became part of the estate of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|ja|興福寺}}, specifically under the Kita-in sub-temple of the Nikaidō lineage. As the estate developed, the worship of water deities grew in importance. In 972 (Tenroku 3), the shrine was relocated to its current site in Tomoda. The current main shrine building was constructed in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the Muromachi period.
=== Divine Ranks === * {{ill|Shōwa (era)|ja|承和}} 7 (840), October 7: Elevated from unranked to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower ({{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本後紀}}) * {{ill|Tian’an|ja|天安}} 3 (859), January 27: Elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}})

== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * '''Main Shrine''' (附 棟札6枚) - Built in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the late Muromachi period. Designated on August 28, 1907.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|102|2676|都祁水分神社本殿}}</ref>
=== Nara Prefectural Designation === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''' * '''Gilded Bronze Mikoshi''' (Portable Shrine) - Constructed in 1456 (Kōshō 3). Designated on March 31, 1975.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Official Website).</ref>

'''Tsuge Mikumari Shrine''' (都祁水分神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (major shrine) and formerly held the rank of a [[prefectural shrine]] under the old shrine ranking system.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following three deities: * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ}} * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|lt=Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|天水分神}} (Heavenly Water Distribution Deity) * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|lt=Kuni-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|国水分神}} (Earthly Water Distribution Deity)
== History ==
=== Early History === The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is traditionally believed to have been established during the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. Initially located at the site of {{ill|Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine|ja|都祁山口神社}}, it was later moved to its current location, believed to have been used as the Horigoe Temporary Palace during the reign of Emperor Shōmu. The area served as a lodging site for the imperial princesses who were priestesses of the Ise Grand Shrine during the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}.
The shrine was listed as a major shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} and received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-matsuri|ja|月次祭}} and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|ja|新嘗祭}}.
=== Medieval Period === During the medieval period, the shrine's lands became part of the estate of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|ja|興福寺}}, specifically under the Kita-in sub-temple of the Nikaidō lineage. As the estate developed, the worship of water deities grew in importance. In 972 (Tenroku 3), the shrine was relocated to its current site in Tomoda. The current main shrine building was constructed in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the Muromachi period.
=== Divine Ranks === * {{ill|Shōwa (era)|ja|承和}} 7 (840), October 7: Elevated from unranked to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower ({{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本後紀}}) * {{ill|Tian’an|ja|天安}} 3 (859), January 27: Elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}})
== Grounds == <gallery> File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Maiden.jpg|舞殿 (Dance Hall) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Haiden.jpg|拝殿 (Worship Hall) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 (Main Shrine, Important Cultural Property) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Chokushaden.jpg|直会殿 (Banquet Hall) </gallery>
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * '''Main Shrine''' (附 棟札6枚) - Built in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the late Muromachi period. Designated on August 28, 1907.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|102|2676|都祁水分神社本殿}}</ref>
=== Nara Prefectural Designation === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''' * '''Gilded Bronze Mikoshi''' (Portable Shrine) - Constructed in 1456 (Kōshō 3). Designated on March 31, 1975.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Official Website).</ref>
== See Also == * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|fr}} * {{ill|Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|大和国の式内社一覧}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuge-mikumari-jinja}} * [http://www.tsugemikumari.or.jp Official Website of Tsuge Mikumari Shrine] * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=20501 Tsuge Mikumari Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program: Shinto and Shrine Historical Resources Collection

'''Tsuge Mikumari Shrine''' (都祁水分神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (major shrine) and formerly held the rank of a [[prefectural shrine]] under the old shrine ranking system.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following three deities: * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ}} * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|lt=Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|天水分神}} (Heavenly Water Distribution Deity) * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|lt=Kuni-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|国水分神}} (Earthly Water Distribution Deity)
== History ==
=== Early History === The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is traditionally believed to have been established during the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. Initially located at the site of {{ill|Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine|ja|都祁山口神社}}, it was later moved to its current location, believed to have been used as the Horigoe Temporary Palace during the reign of Emperor Shōmu. The area served as a lodging site for the imperial princesses who were priestesses of the Ise Grand Shrine during the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}.
The shrine was listed as a major shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} and received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭}} and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|ja|新嘗祭}}.
=== Medieval Period === During the medieval period, the shrine's lands became part of the estate of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|ja|興福寺}}, specifically under the Kita-in sub-temple of the Nikaidō lineage. As the estate developed, the worship of water deities grew in importance. In 972 (Tenroku 3), the shrine was relocated to its current site in Tomoda. The current main shrine building was constructed in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the Muromachi period.
=== Divine Ranks === * {{ill|Shōwa (era)|ja|承和}} 7 (840), October 7: Elevated from unranked to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower ({{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本後紀}}) * {{ill|Tian’an|ja|天安}} 3 (859), January 27: Elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}})

=== Nara Prefectural Designation === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''' * '''Gilded Bronze Mikoshi''' (Portable Shrine) - Constructed in 1456 (Kōshō 3). Designated on March 31, 1975.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Official Website).</ref>

'''Tsuge Mikumari Shrine''' (都祁水分神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (major shrine) and formerly held the rank of a [[prefectural shrine]] under the old shrine ranking system.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following three deities: * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ}} * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|lt=Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|天水分神}} (Heavenly Water Distribution Deity) * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|lt=Kuni-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|国水分神}} (Earthly Water Distribution Deity)
== History ==
=== Early History === The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is traditionally believed to have been established during the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. Initially located at the site of {{ill|Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine|ja|都祁山口神社}}, it was later moved to its current location, believed to have been used as the Horigoe Temporary Palace during the reign of Emperor Shōmu. The area served as a lodging site for the imperial princesses who were priestesses of the Ise Grand Shrine during the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}.
The shrine was listed as a major shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} and received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭}} and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|ja|新嘗祭}}.
=== Medieval Period === During the medieval period, the shrine's lands became part of the estate of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|ja|興福寺}}, specifically under the Kita-in sub-temple of the Nikaidō lineage. As the estate developed, the worship of water deities grew in importance. In 972 (Tenroku 3), the shrine was relocated to its current site in Tomoda. The current main shrine building was constructed in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the Muromachi period.
=== Divine Ranks === * {{ill|Shōwa (era)|ja|承和}} 7 (840), October 7: Elevated from unranked to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower ({{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本後紀}}) * {{ill|Tian’an|ja|天安}} 3 (859), January 27: Elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}})
== Grounds == <gallery> File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Maiden.jpg|舞殿 (Dance Hall) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Haiden.jpg|拝殿 (Worship Hall) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 (Main Shrine, Important Cultural Property) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Chokushaden.jpg|直会殿 (Banquet Hall) </gallery>
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * '''Main Shrine''' (附 棟札6枚) - Built in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the late Muromachi period. Designated on August 28, 1907.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|102|2676|都祁水分神社本殿}}</ref>
=== Nara Prefectural Designation === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''' * '''Gilded Bronze Mikoshi''' (Portable Shrine) - Constructed in 1456 (Kōshō 3). Designated on March 31, 1975.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Official Website).</ref>
== See Also == * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|fr}} * {{ill|Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|大和国の式内社一覧}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuge-mikumari-jinja}} * [http://www.tsugemikumari.or.jp Official Website of Tsuge Mikumari Shrine] * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=20501 Tsuge Mikumari Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program: Shinto and Shrine Historical Resources Collection

'''Tsuge Mikumari Shrine''' (都祁水分神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (major shrine) and formerly held the rank of a [[prefectural shrine]] under the old shrine ranking system.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following three deities: * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ}} * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|lt=Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|天水分神}} (Heavenly Water Distribution Deity) * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|lt=Kuni-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|国水分神}} (Earthly Water Distribution Deity)
== History ==
=== Early History === The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is traditionally believed to have been established during the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. Initially located at the site of {{ill|Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine|ja|都祁山口神社}}, it was later moved to its current location, believed to have been used as the Horigoe Temporary Palace during the reign of Emperor Shōmu. The area served as a lodging site for the imperial princesses who were priestesses of the Ise Grand Shrine during the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}.
The shrine was listed as a major shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} and received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭}} and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|ja|新嘗祭}}.
=== Medieval Period === During the medieval period, the shrine's lands became part of the estate of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|ja|興福寺}}, specifically under the Kita-in sub-temple of the Nikaidō lineage. As the estate developed, the worship of water deities grew in importance. In 972 (Tenroku 3), the shrine was relocated to its current site in Tomoda. The current main shrine building was constructed in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the Muromachi period.
=== Divine Ranks === * {{ill|Shōwa (era)|ja|承和}} 7 (840), October 7: Elevated from unranked to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower ({{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本後紀}}) * {{ill|Tian’an|ja|天安}} 3 (859), January 27: Elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}})
== Grounds == <gallery> File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Maiden.jpg|舞殿 (Dance Hall) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Haiden.jpg|拝殿 (Worship Hall) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 (Main Shrine, Important Cultural Property) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Chokushaden.jpg|直会殿 (Banquet Hall) </gallery>
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * '''Main Shrine''' (附 棟札6枚) - Built in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the late Muromachi period. Designated on August 28, 1907.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|102|2676|都祁水分神社本殿}}</ref>
=== Nara Prefectural Designation === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''' * '''Gilded Bronze Mikoshi''' (Portable Shrine) - Constructed in 1456 (Kōshō 3). Designated on March 31, 1975.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Official Website).</ref>
== See Also == * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|fr}} * {{ill|Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|大和国の式内社一覧}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuge-mikumari-jinja}} * [http://www.tsugemikumari.or.jp Official Website of Tsuge Mikumari Shrine] * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=20501 Tsuge Mikumari Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program: Shinto and Shrine Historical Resources Collection

revision.text:
'''Tsuge Mikumari Shrine''' (都祁水分神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (major shrine) and formerly held the rank of a [[prefectural shrine]] under the old shrine ranking system.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following three deities: * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ}} * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|lt=Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|天水分神}} (Heavenly Water Distribution Deity) * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|lt=Kuni-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|国水分神}} (Earthly Water Distribution Deity)
== History ==
=== Early History === The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is traditionally believed to have been established during the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. Initially located at the site of {{ill|Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine|ja|都祁山口神社}}, it was later moved to its current location, believed to have been used as the Horigoe Temporary Palace during the reign of Emperor Shōmu. The area served as a lodging site for the imperial princesses who were priestesses of the Ise Grand Shrine during the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}.
The shrine was listed as a major shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} and received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭}} and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|ja|新嘗祭}}.
=== Medieval Period === During the medieval period, the shrine's lands became part of the estate of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|ja|興福寺}}, specifically under the Kita-in sub-temple of the Nikaidō lineage. As the estate developed, the worship of water deities grew in importance. In 972 (Tenroku 3), the shrine was relocated to its current site in Tomoda. The current main shrine building was constructed in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the Muromachi period.
=== Divine Ranks === * {{ill|Shōwa (era)|ja|承和}} 7 (840), October 7: Elevated from unranked to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower ({{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本後紀}}) * {{ill|Tian’an|ja|天安}} 3 (859), January 27: Elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}})
== Grounds == <gallery> File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Maiden.jpg|舞殿 (Dance Hall) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Haiden.jpg|拝殿 (Worship Hall) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 (Main Shrine, Important Cultural Property) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Chokushaden.jpg|直会殿 (Banquet Hall) </gallery>
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * '''Main Shrine''' (附 棟札6枚) - Built in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the late Muromachi period. Designated on August 28, 1907.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|102|2676|都祁水分神社本殿}}</ref>
=== Nara Prefectural Designation === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''' * '''Gilded Bronze Mikoshi''' (Portable Shrine) - Constructed in 1456 (Kōshō 3). Designated on March 31, 1975.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Official Website).</ref>
== See Also == * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|fr}} * {{ill|Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|大和国の式内社一覧}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuge-mikumari-jinja}} * [http://www.tsugemikumari.or.jp Official Website of Tsuge Mikumari Shrine] * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=20501 Tsuge Mikumari Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program: Shinto and Shrine Historical Resources Collection

'''Tsuge Mikumari Shrine''' (都祁水分神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (major shrine) and formerly held the rank of a [[prefectural shrine]] under the old shrine ranking system.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following three deities: * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ}} * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|lt=Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|天水分神}} (Heavenly Water Distribution Deity) * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|lt=Kuni-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|国水分神}} (Earthly Water Distribution Deity)
== History ==
=== Early History === The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is traditionally believed to have been established during the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. Initially located at the site of {{ill|Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine|ja|都祁山口神社}}, it was later moved to its current location, believed to have been used as the Horigoe Temporary Palace during the reign of Emperor Shōmu. The area served as a lodging site for the imperial princesses who were priestesses of the Ise Grand Shrine during the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}.
The shrine was listed as a major shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} and received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭}} and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|ja|新嘗祭}}.
=== Medieval Period === During the medieval period, the shrine's lands became part of the estate of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|ja|興福寺}}, specifically under the Kita-in sub-temple of the Nikaidō lineage. As the estate developed, the worship of water deities grew in importance. In 972 (Tenroku 3), the shrine was relocated to its current site in Tomoda. The current main shrine building was constructed in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the Muromachi period.
=== Divine Ranks === * {{ill|Shōwa (era)|ja|承和}} 7 (840), October 7: Elevated from unranked to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower ({{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本後紀}}) * {{ill|Tian’an|ja|天安}} 3 (859), January 27: Elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}})
== Grounds == <gallery> File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Maiden.jpg|舞殿 (Dance Hall) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Haiden.jpg|拝殿 (Worship Hall) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 (Main Shrine, Important Cultural Property) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Chokushaden.jpg|直会殿 (Banquet Hall) </gallery>
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * '''Main Shrine''' (附 棟札6枚) - Built in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the late Muromachi period. Designated on August 28, 1907.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|102|2676|都祁水分神社本殿}}</ref>
=== Nara Prefectural Designation === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''' * '''Gilded Bronze Mikoshi''' (Portable Shrine) - Constructed in 1456 (Kōshō 3). Designated on March 31, 1975.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Official Website).</ref>
== See Also == * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|fr}} * {{ill|Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|大和国の式内社一覧}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuge-mikumari-jinja}} * [http://www.tsugemikumari.or.jp Official Website of Tsuge Mikumari Shrine] * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=20501 Tsuge Mikumari Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program: Shinto and Shrine Historical Resources Collection

=== Early History === The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is traditionally believed to have been established during the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. Initially located at the site of {{ill|Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine|ja|都祁山口神社}}, it was later moved to its current location, believed to have been used as the Horigoe Temporary Palace during the reign of Emperor Shōmu. The area served as a lodging site for the imperial princesses who were priestesses of the Ise Grand Shrine during the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}.
The shrine was listed as a major shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} and received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭}} and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|ja|新嘗祭}}.
=== Medieval Period === During the medieval period, the shrine's lands became part of the estate of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|ja|興福寺}}, specifically under the Kita-in sub-temple of the Nikaidō lineage. As the estate developed, the worship of water deities grew in importance. In 972 (Tenroku 3), the shrine was relocated to its current site in Tomoda. The current main shrine building was constructed in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the Muromachi period.
=== Divine Ranks === * {{ill|Shōwa (era)|ja|承和}} 7 (840), October 7: Elevated from unranked to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower ({{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本後紀}}) * {{ill|Tian’an|ja|天安}} 3 (859), January 27: Elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}})

=== Nara Prefectural Designation === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''' ** '''Gilded Bronze Mikoshi''' (Portable Shrine) - Constructed in 1456 (Kōshō 3). Designated on March 31, 1975.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Official Website).</ref>

'''Tsuge Mikumari Shrine''' (都祁水分神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (major shrine) and formerly held the rank of a [[prefectural shrine]] under the old shrine ranking system.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following three deities: * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ}} * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|lt=Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|天水分神}} (Heavenly Water Distribution Deity) * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|lt=Kuni-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|国水分神}} (Earthly Water Distribution Deity)
== History ==
=== Early History === The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is traditionally believed to have been established during the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. Initially located at the site of {{ill|Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine|ja|都祁山口神社}}, it was later moved to its current location, believed to have been used as the Horigoe Temporary Palace during the reign of Emperor Shōmu. The area served as a lodging site for the imperial princesses who were priestesses of the Ise Grand Shrine during the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}.
The shrine was listed as a major shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} and received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭}} and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|ja|新嘗祭}}.
=== Medieval Period === During the medieval period, the shrine's lands became part of the estate of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|ja|興福寺}}, specifically under the Kita-in sub-temple of the Nikaidō lineage. As the estate developed, the worship of water deities grew in importance. In 972 (Tenroku 3), the shrine was relocated to its current site in Tomoda. The current main shrine building was constructed in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the Muromachi period.
=== Divine Ranks === * {{ill|Shōwa (era)|ja|承和}} 7 (840), October 7: Elevated from unranked to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower ({{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本後紀}}) * {{ill|Tian’an|ja|天安}} 3 (859), January 27: Elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}})

== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * '''Main Shrine''' (附 棟札6枚) - Built in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the late Muromachi period. Designated on August 28, 1907.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|102|2676|都祁水分神社本殿}}</ref>
=== Nara Prefectural Designation === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''' ** '''Gilded Bronze Mikoshi''' (Portable Shrine) - Constructed in 1456 (Kōshō 3). Designated on March 31, 1975.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Official Website).</ref>
== See Also == * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|fr}} * {{ill|Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "↵↵== See Also ==" to "== See Also =="
revision.text:
'''Tsuge Mikumari Shrine''' (都祁水分神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (major shrine) and formerly held the rank of a [[prefectural shrine]] under the old shrine ranking system.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following three deities: * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ}} * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|lt=Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|天水分神}} (Heavenly Water Distribution Deity) * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|lt=Kuni-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|国水分神}} (Earthly Water Distribution Deity)
== History ==
=== Early History === The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is traditionally believed to have been established during the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. Initially located at the site of {{ill|Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine|ja|都祁山口神社}}, it was later moved to its current location, believed to have been used as the Horigoe Temporary Palace during the reign of Emperor Shōmu. The area served as a lodging site for the imperial princesses who were priestesses of the Ise Grand Shrine during the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}.
The shrine was listed as a major shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} and received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭}} and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|ja|新嘗祭}}.
=== Medieval Period === During the medieval period, the shrine's lands became part of the estate of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|ja|興福寺}}, specifically under the Kita-in sub-temple of the Nikaidō lineage. As the estate developed, the worship of water deities grew in importance. In 972 (Tenroku 3), the shrine was relocated to its current site in Tomoda. The current main shrine building was constructed in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the Muromachi period.
=== Divine Ranks === * {{ill|Shōwa (era)|ja|承和}} 7 (840), October 7: Elevated from unranked to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower ({{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本後紀}}) * {{ill|Tian’an|ja|天安}} 3 (859), January 27: Elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}})
== Grounds == <gallery> File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Maiden.jpg|舞殿 (Dance Hall) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Haiden.jpg|拝殿 (Worship Hall) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 (Main Shrine, Important Cultural Property) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Chokushaden.jpg|直会殿 (Banquet Hall) </gallery>
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * '''Main Shrine''' (附 棟札6枚) - Built in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the late Muromachi period. Designated on August 28, 1907.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|102|2676|都祁水分神社本殿}}</ref>
=== Nara Prefectural Designation === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''' ** '''Gilded Bronze Mikoshi''' (Portable Shrine) - Constructed in 1456 (Kōshō 3). Designated on March 31, 1975.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Official Website).</ref>== See Also == * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|fr}} * {{ill|Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuge-mikumari-jinja}} * [http://www.tsugemikumari.or.jp Official Website of Tsuge Mikumari Shrine] * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=20501 Tsuge Mikumari Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program: Shinto and Shrine Historical Resources Collection
{{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha shrines]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Nara City]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsuge Mikumari Shrine]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsuge Mikumari Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsuge Mikumari Shrine]][[Category:11]] <!--Tsuge Mikumari Shrine-->[[Category:Tsuge Mikumari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] {{Munakata Faith}} [[Category:Munakata Faith]] [[Category:s11]][[sn:Tsuge Mikumari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tsuge Mikumari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tsuge Mikumari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tsuge Mikumari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tsuge Mikumari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsuge Mikumari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsuge Mikumari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsuge Mikumari Shrine]][[sn:suge Mikumari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsuge Mikumari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:suge Mikumari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Tsuge Mikumari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsuge Mikumari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsuge Mikumari Shrine]][[Category:11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsuge Mikumari Shrine}}[[ja:都祁水分神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuge Mikumari Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

revision.text:
'''Tsuge Mikumari Shrine''' (都祁水分神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (major shrine) and formerly held the rank of a [[prefectural shrine]] under the old shrine ranking system.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following three deities: * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ}} * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|lt=Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|天水分神}} (Heavenly Water Distribution Deity) * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|lt=Kuni-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|国水分神}} (Earthly Water Distribution Deity)
== History ==
=== Early History === The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is traditionally believed to have been established during the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. Initially located at the site of {{ill|Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine|ja|都祁山口神社}}, it was later moved to its current location, believed to have been used as the Horigoe Temporary Palace during the reign of Emperor Shōmu. The area served as a lodging site for the imperial princesses who were priestesses of the Ise Grand Shrine during the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}.
The shrine was listed as a major shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} and received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|ja|新嘗祭}}.
=== Medieval Period === During the medieval period, the shrine's lands became part of the estate of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|ja|興福寺}}, specifically under the Kita-in sub-temple of the Nikaidō lineage. As the estate developed, the worship of water deities grew in importance. In 972 (Tenroku 3), the shrine was relocated to its current site in Tomoda. The current main shrine building was constructed in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the Muromachi period.
=== Divine Ranks === * {{ill|Shōwa (era)|ja|承和}} 7 (840), October 7: Elevated from unranked to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower ({{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本後紀}}) * {{ill|Tian’an|ja|天安}} 3 (859), January 27: Elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}})
== Grounds == <gallery> File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Maiden.jpg|舞殿 (Dance Hall) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Haiden.jpg|拝殿 (Worship Hall) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 (Main Shrine, Important Cultural Property) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Chokushaden.jpg|直会殿 (Banquet Hall) </gallery>
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * '''Main Shrine''' (附 棟札6枚) - Built in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the late Muromachi period. Designated on August 28, 1907.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|102|2676|都祁水分神社本殿}}</ref>
=== Nara Prefectural Designation === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''' ** '''Gilded Bronze Mikoshi''' (Portable Shrine) - Constructed in 1456 (Kōshō 3). Designated on March 31, 1975.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Official Website).</ref> == See Also == * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|fr}} * {{ill|Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuge-mikumari-jinja}} * [http://www.tsugemikumari.or.jp Official Website of Tsuge Mikumari Shrine] * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=20501 Tsuge Mikumari Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program: Shinto and Shrine Historical Resources Collection

revision.comment: Bot: Replaced ill templates with appended |12=draft and created corresponding redirect pages.
revision.text:
'''Tsuge Mikumari Shrine''' (都祁水分神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|12=draft}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|12=draft}} (major shrine) and formerly held the rank of a [[prefectural shrine]] under the old shrine ranking system.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following three deities: * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|lt=Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|天水分神|12=draft}} (Heavenly Water Distribution Deity) * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|lt=Kuni-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|国水分神|12=draft}} (Earthly Water Distribution Deity)
== History ==
=== Early History === The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is traditionally believed to have been established during the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代|12=draft}}. Initially located at the site of {{ill|Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine|ja|都祁山口神社|12=draft}}, it was later moved to its current location, believed to have been used as the Horigoe Temporary Palace during the reign of Emperor Shōmu. The area served as a lodging site for the imperial princesses who were priestesses of the Ise Grand Shrine during the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}}.
The shrine was listed as a major shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft}} and received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=draft}} and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|ja|新嘗祭|12=draft}}.
=== Medieval Period === During the medieval period, the shrine's lands became part of the estate of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|ja|興福寺|12=draft}}, specifically under the Kita-in sub-temple of the Nikaidō lineage. As the estate developed, the worship of water deities grew in importance. In 972 (Tenroku 3), the shrine was relocated to its current site in Tomoda. The current main shrine building was constructed in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the Muromachi period.
=== Divine Ranks === * {{ill|Shōwa (era)|ja|承和|12=draft}} 7 (840), October 7: Elevated from unranked to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower ({{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本後紀|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Tian’an|ja|天安|12=draft}} 3 (859), January 27: Elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft}})
== Grounds == <gallery> File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Maiden.jpg|舞殿 (Dance Hall) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Haiden.jpg|拝殿 (Worship Hall) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 (Main Shrine, Important Cultural Property) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Chokushaden.jpg|直会殿 (Banquet Hall) </gallery>
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * '''Main Shrine''' (附 棟札6枚) - Built in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the late Muromachi period. Designated on August 28, 1907.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|102|2676|都祁水分神社本殿}}</ref>
=== Nara Prefectural Designation === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''' ** '''Gilded Bronze Mikoshi''' (Portable Shrine) - Constructed in 1456 (Kōshō 3). Designated on March 31, 1975.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Official Website).</ref> == See Also == * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|fr|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=draft}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuge-mikumari-jinja}} * [http://www.tsugemikumari.or.jp Official Website of Tsuge Mikumari Shrine] * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=20501 Tsuge Mikumari Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program: Shinto and Shrine Historical Resources Collection
{{Shinto-stub}}
<!--Tsuge Mikumari Shrine-->
{{Munakata Faith}}

=== Nara Prefectural Designation === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''' ** '''Gilded Bronze Mikoshi''' (Portable Shrine) - Constructed in 1456 (Kōshō 3). Designated on March 31, 1975.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Official Website).</ref> == See Also == * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|fr|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=draft}}

{{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha shrines]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Nara City]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tsuge Mikumari Shrine]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Tsuge Mikumari Shrine]] [[sn:suge Mikumari Shrine]] [[ja:都祁水分神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsuge Mikumari Shrine}}

'''Tsuge Mikumari Shrine''' (都祁水分神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|12=draft}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|12=draft}} (major shrine) and formerly held the rank of a [[prefectural shrine]] under the old shrine ranking system.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following three deities: * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|lt=Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|天水分神|12=draft}} (Heavenly Water Distribution Deity) * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|lt=Kuni-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|国水分神|12=draft}} (Earthly Water Distribution Deity)
== History ==
=== Early History === The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is traditionally believed to have been established during the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代|12=draft}}. Initially located at the site of {{ill|Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine|ja|都祁山口神社|12=draft}}, it was later moved to its current location, believed to have been used as the Horigoe Temporary Palace during the reign of Emperor Shōmu. The area served as a lodging site for the imperial princesses who were priestesses of the Ise Grand Shrine during the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}}.
The shrine was listed as a major shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft}} and received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=draft}} and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|ja|新嘗祭|12=draft}}.
=== Medieval Period === During the medieval period, the shrine's lands became part of the estate of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|ja|興福寺|12=draft}}, specifically under the Kita-in sub-temple of the Nikaidō lineage. As the estate developed, the worship of water deities grew in importance. In 972 (Tenroku 3), the shrine was relocated to its current site in Tomoda. The current main shrine building was constructed in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the Muromachi period.
=== Divine Ranks === * {{ill|Shōwa (era)|ja|承和|12=draft}} 7 (840), October 7: Elevated from unranked to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower ({{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本後紀|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Tian’an|ja|天安|12=draft}} 3 (859), January 27: Elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft}})
== Grounds == <gallery> File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Maiden.jpg|舞殿 (Dance Hall) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Haiden.jpg|拝殿 (Worship Hall) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 (Main Shrine, Important Cultural Property) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Chokushaden.jpg|直会殿 (Banquet Hall) </gallery>
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * '''Main Shrine''' (附 棟札6枚) - Built in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the late Muromachi period. Designated on August 28, 1907.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|102|2676|都祁水分神社本殿}}</ref>
=== Nara Prefectural Designation === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''' ** '''Gilded Bronze Mikoshi''' (Portable Shrine) - Constructed in 1456 (Kōshō 3). Designated on March 31, 1975.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Official Website).</ref> == See Also == * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|fr|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=draft}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuge-mikumari-jinja}} * [http://www.tsugemikumari.or.jp Official Website of Tsuge Mikumari Shrine] * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=20501 Tsuge Mikumari Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program: Shinto and Shrine Historical Resources Collection
{{Shinto-stub}} <!--Tsuge Mikumari Shrine--> {{Munakata Faith}}

== Grounds == <gallery> File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Maiden.jpg|舞殿 (Dance Hall) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Haiden.jpg|拝殿 (Worship Hall) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 (Main Shrine, Important Cultural Property) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Chokushaden.jpg|直会殿 (Banquet Hall) </gallery>
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * '''Main Shrine''' (附 棟札6枚) - Built in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the late Muromachi period. Designated on August 28, 1907.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|102|2676|都祁水分神社本殿}}</ref>
=== Nara Prefectural Designation === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''' ** '''Gilded Bronze Mikoshi''' (Portable Shrine) - Constructed in 1456 (Kōshō 3). Designated on March 31, 1975.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Official Website).</ref> == See Also == * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Tsuge-mikumari-jinja]][[ja:都祁水分神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Shaden.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Nara|qid=Q169134}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.59722222222222|N|135.94722222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q22119448]] Wikidata name: Tsuge-mikumari-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tsuge Mikumari Shrine''' (都祁水分神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (major shrine) and formerly held the Rank of a [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Prefectural shrine]] under the old shrine Ranking system.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following three deities: * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|qq=}} * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|lt=Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|天水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}} (Heavenly Water Distribution Deity) * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|lt=Kuni-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|国水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}} (Earthly Water Distribution Deity)
== History ==
=== Early History === The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is traditionally believed to have been established during the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. Initially located at the site of {{ill|Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine|ja|都祁山口神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine|qq=}}, it was later moved to its current location, believed to have been used as the Horigoe Temporary Palace during the reign of Emperor Shōmu. The area served as a lodging site for the imperial princesses who were priestesses of the Ise Grand Shrine during the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]].
The shrine was listed as a major shrine in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] and received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-no-Matsuri]].
=== Medieval Period === During the medieval period, the shrine's lands became part of the estate of [[:en:Kōfuku-ji|Kōfuku-ji]], specifically under the Kita-in sub-temple of the Nikaidō lineage. As the estate developed, the worship of water deities grew in importance. In 972 (Tenroku 3), the shrine was relocated to its current site in Tomoda. The current main shrine building was constructed in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the Muromachi period.
=== Divine Ranks === * [[:en:Jōwa|Shōwa (era)]] 7 (840), October 7: Elevated from unranked to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower ([[:en:Shoku Nihon Kōki|Shoku Nihongi]]) * {{ill|Tian’an|ja|天安|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tian’an|qq=|1=Cheonan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3577288}} 3 (859), January 27: Elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower ([[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]])
== Grounds == <gallery> File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Maiden.jpg|舞殿 (Dance Hall) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Haiden.jpg|拝殿 (Worship Hall) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 (Main Shrine, Important Cultural Property) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Chokushaden.jpg|直会殿 (Banquet Hall) </gallery>
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * '''Main Shrine''' (附 棟札6枚) - Built in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the late Muromachi period. Designated on August 28, 1907.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|102|2676|都祁水分神社本殿}}</ref>
=== Nara Prefectural Designation === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''' ** '''Gilded Bronze Mikoshi''' (Portable Shrine) - Constructed in 1456 (Kōshō 3). Designated on March 31, 1975.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Official Website).</ref> == See Also == * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}} * {{ill|Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuge-mikumari-jinja}} * [http://www.tsugemikumari.or.jp Official Website of Tsuge Mikumari Shrine] * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=20501 Tsuge Mikumari Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program: Shinto and Shrine Historical Resources Collection
