== History ==
=== Early History === The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is traditionally believed to have been established during the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. Initially located at the site of {{ill|Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine|ja|都祁山口神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine|qq=}}, it was later moved to its current location, believed to have been used as the Horigoe Temporary Palace during the reign of Emperor Shōmu. The area served as a lodging site for the imperial princesses who were priestesses of the Ise Grand Shrine during the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]].
The shrine was listed as a major shrine in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] and received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-no-Matsuri]].
=== Medieval Period === During the medieval period, the shrine's lands became part of the estate of [[:en:Kōfuku-ji|Kōfuku-ji]], specifically under the Kita-in sub-temple of the Nikaidō lineage. As the estate developed, the worship of water deities grew in importance. In 972 (Tenroku 3), the shrine was relocated to its current site in Tomoda. The current main shrine building was constructed in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the Muromachi period.
=== Divine Ranks === * [[:en:Jōwa|Shōwa (era)]] 7 (840), October 7: Elevated from unranked to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower ([[:en:Shoku Nihon Kōki|Shoku Nihongi]]) * {{ill|Tian’an|ja|天安|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tian’an|qq=|1=Cheonan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3577288}} 3 (859), January 27: Elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower ([[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]])
== Grounds == <gallery> File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Maiden.jpg|舞殿 (Dance Hall) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Haiden.jpg|拝殿 (Worship Hall) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 (Main Shrine, Important Cultural Property) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Chokushaden.jpg|直会殿 (Banquet Hall) </gallery>
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * '''Main Shrine''' (附 棟札6枚) - Built in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the late Muromachi period. Designated on August 28, 1907.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|102|2676|都祁水分神社本殿}}</ref>
=== Nara Prefectural Designation === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''' ** '''Gilded Bronze Mikoshi''' (Portable Shrine) - Constructed in 1456 (Kōshō 3). Designated on March 31, 1975.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Official Website).</ref> == See Also == * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}} * {{ill|Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

Designated on March 31, 1975.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Official Website).</ref> == See Also == * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}} * {{ill|Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tsuge-mikumari-jinja]][[ja:都祁水分神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Shaden.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.59722222222222|N|135.94722222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q22119448]] Wikidata name: Tsuge-mikumari-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tsuge Mikumari Shrine''' (都祁水分神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (major shrine) and formerly held the Rank of a [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Prefectural shrine]] under the old shrine Ranking system.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following three deities: * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|qq=}} * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|lt=Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|天水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}} (Heavenly Water Distribution Deity) * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|lt=Kuni-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|国水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}} (Earthly Water Distribution Deity)
== History ==
=== Early History === The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is traditionally believed to have been established during the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. Initially located at the site of {{ill|Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine|ja|都祁山口神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine|qq=}}, it was later moved to its current location, believed to have been used as the Horigoe Temporary Palace during the reign of Emperor Shōmu. The area served as a lodging site for the imperial princesses who were priestesses of the Ise Grand Shrine during the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]].
The shrine was listed as a major shrine in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] and received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-no-Matsuri]].
=== Medieval Period === During the medieval period, the shrine's lands became part of the estate of [[:en:Kōfuku-ji|Kōfuku-ji]], specifically under the Kita-in sub-temple of the Nikaidō lineage. As the estate developed, the worship of water deities grew in importance. In 972 (Tenroku 3), the shrine was relocated to its current site in Tomoda. The current main shrine building was constructed in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the Muromachi period.
=== Divine Ranks === * [[:en:Jōwa|Shōwa (era)]] 7 (840), October 7: Elevated from unranked to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower ([[:en:Shoku Nihon Kōki|Shoku Nihongi]]) * {{ill|Tian’an|ja|天安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tian’an|1=Cheonan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3577288}} 3 (859), January 27: Elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower ([[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]])
== Grounds == <gallery> File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Maiden.jpg|舞殿 (Dance Hall) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Haiden.jpg|拝殿 (Worship Hall) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 (Main Shrine, Important Cultural Property) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Chokushaden.jpg|直会殿 (Banquet Hall) </gallery>
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * '''Main Shrine''' (附 棟札6枚) - Built in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the late Muromachi period. Designated on August 28, 1907.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|102|2676|都祁水分神社本殿}}</ref>
=== Nara Prefectural Designation === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''' ** '''Gilded Bronze Mikoshi''' (Portable Shrine) - Constructed in 1456 (Kōshō 3). Designated on March 31, 1975.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Official Website).</ref> == See Also == * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}} * {{ill|Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuge-mikumari-jinja}} * [http://www.tsugemikumari.or.jp Official Website of Tsuge Mikumari Shrine] * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=20501 Tsuge Mikumari Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program: Shinto and Shrine Historical Resources Collection

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tsuge-mikumari-jinja]][[ja:都祁水分神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Shaden.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.59722222222222|N|135.94722222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q22119448]] Wikidata name: Tsuge-mikumari-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tsuge Mikumari Shrine''' (都祁水分神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (major shrine) and formerly held the Rank of a [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Prefectural shrine]] under the old shrine Ranking system.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following three deities: * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|qq=}} * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|lt=Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|天水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}} (Heavenly Water Distribution Deity) * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|lt=Kuni-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|国水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}} (Earthly Water Distribution Deity)
== History ==
=== Early History === The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is traditionally believed to have been established during the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. Initially located at the site of {{ill|Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine|ja|都祁山口神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine|qq=}}, it was later moved to its current location, believed to have been used as the Horigoe Temporary Palace during the reign of Emperor Shōmu. The area served as a lodging site for the imperial princesses who were priestesses of the Ise Grand Shrine during the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]].
The shrine was listed as a major shrine in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] and received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-no-Matsuri]].
=== Medieval Period === During the medieval period, the shrine's lands became part of the estate of [[:en:Kōfuku-ji|Kōfuku-ji]], specifically under the Kita-in sub-temple of the Nikaidō lineage. As the estate developed, the worship of water deities grew in importance. In 972 (Tenroku 3), the shrine was relocated to its current site in Tomoda. The current main shrine building was constructed in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the Muromachi period.
=== Divine Ranks === * [[:en:Jōwa|Shōwa (era)]] 7 (840), October 7: Elevated from unranked to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower ([[:en:Shoku Nihon Kōki|Shoku Nihongi]]) * {{ill|Tian’an|ja|天安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tian’an|1=Cheonan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3577288}} 3 (859), January 27: Elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower ([[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]])

=== Nara Prefectural Designation === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''' ** '''Gilded Bronze Mikoshi''' (Portable Shrine) - Constructed in 1456 (Kōshō 3). Designated on March 31, 1975.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Official Website).</ref> == See Also == * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}} * {{ill|Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tsuge-mikumari-jinja]][[ja:都祁水分神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Shaden.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.59722222222222|N|135.94722222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q22119448]] Wikidata name: Tsuge-mikumari-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tsuge Mikumari Shrine''' (都祁水分神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (major shrine) and formerly held the Rank of a [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Prefectural shrine]] under the old shrine Ranking system.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following three deities: * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|qq=}} * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|lt=Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|天水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}} (Heavenly Water Distribution Deity) * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|lt=Kuni-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|国水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}} (Earthly Water Distribution Deity)
== History ==
=== Early History === The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is traditionally believed to have been established during the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. Initially located at the site of {{ill|Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine|ja|都祁山口神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine|qq=}}, it was later moved to its current location, believed to have been used as the Horigoe Temporary Palace during the reign of Emperor Shōmu. The area served as a lodging site for the imperial princesses who were priestesses of the Ise Grand Shrine during the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]].
The shrine was listed as a major shrine in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] and received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-no-Matsuri]].
=== Medieval Period === During the medieval period, the shrine's lands became part of the estate of [[:en:Kōfuku-ji|Kōfuku-ji]], specifically under the Kita-in sub-temple of the Nikaidō lineage. As the estate developed, the worship of water deities grew in importance. In 972 (Tenroku 3), the shrine was relocated to its current site in Tomoda. The current main shrine building was constructed in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the Muromachi period.
=== Divine Ranks === * [[:en:Jōwa|Shōwa (era)]] 7 (840), October 7: Elevated from unranked to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower ([[:en:Shoku Nihon Kōki|Shoku Nihongi]]) * {{ill|Tian’an|ja|天安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tian’an|1=Cheonan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3577288}} 3 (859), January 27: Elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower ([[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]])

== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * '''Main Shrine''' (附 棟札6枚) - Built in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the late Muromachi period. Designated on August 28, 1907.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|102|2676|都祁水分神社本殿}}</ref>
=== Nara Prefectural Designation === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''' ** '''Gilded Bronze Mikoshi''' (Portable Shrine) - Constructed in 1456 (Kōshō 3). Designated on March 31, 1975.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Official Website).</ref> == See Also == * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}} * {{ill|Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tsuge-mikumari-jinja]][[ja:都祁水分神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Shaden.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.59722222222222|N|135.94722222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q22119448]] Wikidata name: Tsuge-mikumari-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tsuge Mikumari Shrine''' (都祁水分神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (major shrine) and formerly held the Rank of a [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Prefectural shrine]] under the old shrine Ranking system.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following three deities: * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|qq=}} * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|lt=Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|天水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}} (Heavenly Water Distribution Deity) * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|lt=Kuni-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|国水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}} (Earthly Water Distribution Deity)
== History ==
=== Early History === The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is traditionally believed to have been established during the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. Initially located at the site of {{ill|Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine|ja|都祁山口神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine|qq=}}, it was later moved to its current location, believed to have been used as the Horigoe Temporary Palace during the reign of Emperor Shōmu. The area served as a lodging site for the imperial princesses who were priestesses of the Ise Grand Shrine during the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]].
The shrine was listed as a major shrine in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] and received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-no-Matsuri]].
=== Medieval Period === During the medieval period, the shrine's lands became part of the estate of [[:en:Kōfuku-ji|Kōfuku-ji]], specifically under the Kita-in sub-temple of the Nikaidō lineage. As the estate developed, the worship of water deities grew in importance. In 972 (Tenroku 3), the shrine was relocated to its current site in Tomoda. The current main shrine building was constructed in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the Muromachi period.
=== Divine Ranks === * [[:en:Jōwa|Shōwa (era)]] 7 (840), October 7: Elevated from unranked to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower ([[:en:Shoku Nihon Kōki|Shoku Nihongi]]) * {{ill|Tian’an|ja|天安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tian’an|1=Cheonan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3577288}} 3 (859), January 27: Elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower ([[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]])

== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * '''Main Shrine''' (附 棟札6枚) - Built in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the late Muromachi period. Designated on August 28, 1907.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|102|2676|都祁水分神社本殿}}</ref>
=== Nara Prefectural Designation === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''' ** '''Gilded Bronze Mikoshi''' (Portable Shrine) - Constructed in 1456 (Kōshō 3). Designated on March 31, 1975.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Official Website).</ref> == See Also == * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}} * {{ill|Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

Designated on March 31, 1975.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Official Website).</ref> == See Also == * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}} * {{ill|Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tsuge-mikumari-jinja]][[ja:都祁水分神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Shaden.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.59722222222222|N|135.94722222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q22119448]] Wikidata name: Tsuge-mikumari-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tsuge Mikumari Shrine''' (都祁水分神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (major shrine) and formerly held the Rank of a [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Prefectural shrine]] under the old shrine Ranking system.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following three deities: * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|qq=}} * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|lt=Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|天水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}} (Heavenly Water Distribution Deity) * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|lt=Kuni-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|国水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}} (Earthly Water Distribution Deity)
== History ==
=== Early History === The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is traditionally believed to have been established during the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. Initially located at the site of {{ill|Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine|ja|都祁山口神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine|qq=}}, it was later moved to its current location, believed to have been used as the Horigoe Temporary Palace during the reign of Emperor Shōmu. The area served as a lodging site for the imperial princesses who were priestesses of the Ise Grand Shrine during the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]].
The shrine was listed as a major shrine in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] and received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-no-Matsuri]].
=== Medieval Period === During the medieval period, the shrine's lands became part of the estate of [[:en:Kōfuku-ji|Kōfuku-ji]], specifically under the Kita-in sub-temple of the Nikaidō lineage. As the estate developed, the worship of water deities grew in importance. In 972 (Tenroku 3), the shrine was relocated to its current site in Tomoda. The current main shrine building was constructed in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the Muromachi period.
=== Divine Ranks === * [[:en:Jōwa|Shōwa (era)]] 7 (840), October 7: Elevated from unranked to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower ([[:en:Shoku Nihon Kōki|Shoku Nihongi]]) * {{ill|Tian’an|ja|天安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tian’an|1=Cheonan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3577288}} 3 (859), January 27: Elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower ([[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]])
== Grounds == <gallery> File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Maiden.jpg|舞殿 (Dance Hall) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Haiden.jpg|拝殿 (Worship Hall) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 (Main Shrine, Important Cultural Property) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Chokushaden.jpg|直会殿 (Banquet Hall) </gallery>
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * '''Main Shrine''' (附 棟札6枚) - Built in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the late Muromachi period. Designated on August 28, 1907.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|102|2676|都祁水分神社本殿}}</ref>
=== Nara Prefectural Designation === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''' ** '''Gilded Bronze Mikoshi''' (Portable Shrine) - Constructed in 1456 (Kōshō 3). Designated on March 31, 1975.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Official Website).</ref> == See Also == * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}} * {{ill|Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuge-mikumari-jinja}} * [http://www.tsugemikumari.or.jp Official Website of Tsuge Mikumari Shrine] * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=20501 Tsuge Mikumari Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program: Shinto and Shrine Historical Resources Collection

=== Nara Prefectural Designation === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''' ** '''Gilded Bronze Mikoshi''' (Portable Shrine) - Constructed in 1456 (Kōshō 3). Designated on March 31, 1975.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Official Website).</ref> == See Also == * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}} * {{ill|Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tsuge-mikumari-jinja]][[ja:都祁水分神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Shaden.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.59722222222222|N|135.94722222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q22119448]] Wikidata name: Tsuge-mikumari-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tsuge Mikumari Shrine''' (都祁水分神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (major shrine) and formerly held the Rank of a [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Prefectural shrine]] under the old shrine Ranking system.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following three deities: * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|qq=}} * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|lt=Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|天水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}} (Heavenly Water Distribution Deity) * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|lt=Kuni-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|国水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}} (Earthly Water Distribution Deity)
== History ==
=== Early History === The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is traditionally believed to have been established during the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. Initially located at the site of {{ill|Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine|ja|都祁山口神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine|qq=}}, it was later moved to its current location, believed to have been used as the Horigoe Temporary Palace during the reign of Emperor Shōmu. The area served as a lodging site for the imperial princesses who were priestesses of the Ise Grand Shrine during the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]].
The shrine was listed as a major shrine in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] and received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-no-Matsuri]].
=== Medieval Period === During the medieval period, the shrine's lands became part of the estate of [[:en:Kōfuku-ji|Kōfuku-ji]], specifically under the Kita-in sub-temple of the Nikaidō lineage. As the estate developed, the worship of water deities grew in importance. In 972 (Tenroku 3), the shrine was relocated to its current site in Tomoda. The current main shrine building was constructed in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the Muromachi period.
=== Divine Ranks === * [[:en:Jōwa|Shōwa (era)]] 7 (840), October 7: Elevated from unranked to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower ([[:en:Shoku Nihon Kōki|Shoku Nihongi]]) * {{ill|Tian’an|ja|天安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tian’an|1=Cheonan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3577288}} 3 (859), January 27: Elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower ([[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]])
== Grounds == <gallery> File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Maiden.jpg|舞殿 (Dance Hall) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Haiden.jpg|拝殿 (Worship Hall) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 (Main Shrine, Important Cultural Property) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Chokushaden.jpg|直会殿 (Banquet Hall) </gallery>
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * '''Main Shrine''' (附 棟札6枚) - Built in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the late Muromachi period. Designated on August 28, 1907.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|102|2676|都祁水分神社本殿}}</ref>
=== Nara Prefectural Designation === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''' ** '''Gilded Bronze Mikoshi''' (Portable Shrine) - Constructed in 1456 (Kōshō 3). Designated on March 31, 1975.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Official Website).</ref> == See Also == * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}} * {{ill|Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuge-mikumari-jinja}} * [http://www.tsugemikumari.or.jp Official Website of Tsuge Mikumari Shrine] * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=20501 Tsuge Mikumari Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program: Shinto and Shrine Historical Resources Collection

Designated on March 31, 1975.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Official Website).</ref> == See Also == * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}} * {{ill|Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Tsuge-mikumari-jinja]][[ja:都祁水分神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tsuge-mikumari-jinja]][[ja:都祁水分神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Shaden.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.59722222222222|N|135.94722222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q22119448]] Wikidata name: Tsuge-mikumari-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tsuge Mikumari Shrine''' (都祁水分神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (major shrine) and formerly held the Rank of a [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Prefectural shrine]] under the old shrine Ranking system.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following three deities: * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|qq=}} * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|lt=Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|天水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}} (Heavenly Water Distribution Deity) * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|lt=Kuni-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|国水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}} (Earthly Water Distribution Deity)
== History ==
=== Early History === The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is traditionally believed to have been established during the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. Initially located at the site of {{ill|Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine|ja|都祁山口神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine|qq=}}, it was later moved to its current location, believed to have been used as the Horigoe Temporary Palace during the reign of Emperor Shōmu. The area served as a lodging site for the imperial princesses who were priestesses of the Ise Grand Shrine during the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]].
The shrine was listed as a major shrine in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] and received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-no-Matsuri]].
=== Medieval Period === During the medieval period, the shrine's lands became part of the estate of [[:en:Kōfuku-ji|Kōfuku-ji]], specifically under the Kita-in sub-temple of the Nikaidō lineage. As the estate developed, the worship of water deities grew in importance. In 972 (Tenroku 3), the shrine was relocated to its current site in Tomoda. The current main shrine building was constructed in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the Muromachi period.
=== Divine Ranks === * [[:en:Jōwa|Shōwa (era)]] 7 (840), October 7: Elevated from unranked to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower ([[:en:Shoku Nihon Kōki|Shoku Nihongi]]) * {{ill|Tian’an|ja|天安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tian’an|1=Cheonan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3577288}} 3 (859), January 27: Elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower ([[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]])
== Grounds == <gallery> File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Maiden.jpg|舞殿 (Dance Hall) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Haiden.jpg|拝殿 (Worship Hall) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 (Main Shrine, Important Cultural Property) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Chokushaden.jpg|直会殿 (Banquet Hall) </gallery>
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * '''Main Shrine''' (附 棟札6枚) - Built in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the late Muromachi period. Designated on August 28, 1907.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|102|2676|都祁水分神社本殿}}</ref>
=== Nara Prefectural Designation === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''' ** '''Gilded Bronze Mikoshi''' (Portable Shrine) - Constructed in 1456 (Kōshō 3). Designated on March 31, 1975.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Official Website).</ref> == See Also == * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}} * {{ill|Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuge-mikumari-jinja}} * [http://www.tsugemikumari.or.jp Official Website of Tsuge Mikumari Shrine] * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=20501 Tsuge Mikumari Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program: Shinto and Shrine Historical Resources Collection

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Tsuge-mikumari-jinja]][[ja:都祁水分神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Tsuge-mikumari-jinja]][[ja:都祁水分神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tsuge-mikumari-jinja]][[ja:都祁水分神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Shaden.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.59722222222222|N|135.94722222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q22119448]] Wikidata name: Tsuge-mikumari-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tsuge Mikumari Shrine''' (都祁水分神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (major shrine) and formerly held the Rank of a [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Prefectural shrine]] under the old shrine Ranking system.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following three deities: * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|qq=}} * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|lt=Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|天水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}} (Heavenly Water Distribution Deity) * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|lt=Kuni-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|国水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}} (Earthly Water Distribution Deity)
== History ==
=== Early History === The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is traditionally believed to have been established during the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. Initially located at the site of {{ill|Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine|ja|都祁山口神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine|qq=}}, it was later moved to its current location, believed to have been used as the Horigoe Temporary Palace during the reign of Emperor Shōmu. The area served as a lodging site for the imperial princesses who were priestesses of the Ise Grand Shrine during the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]].
The shrine was listed as a major shrine in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] and received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-no-Matsuri]].
=== Medieval Period === During the medieval period, the shrine's lands became part of the estate of [[:en:Kōfuku-ji|Kōfuku-ji]], specifically under the Kita-in sub-temple of the Nikaidō lineage. As the estate developed, the worship of water deities grew in importance. In 972 (Tenroku 3), the shrine was relocated to its current site in Tomoda. The current main shrine building was constructed in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the Muromachi period.
=== Divine Ranks === * [[:en:Jōwa|Shōwa (era)]] 7 (840), October 7: Elevated from unranked to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower ([[:en:Shoku Nihon Kōki|Shoku Nihongi]]) * {{ill|Tian’an|ja|天安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tian’an|1=Cheonan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3577288}} 3 (859), January 27: Elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower ([[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]])
== Grounds == <gallery> File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Maiden.jpg|舞殿 (Dance Hall) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Haiden.jpg|拝殿 (Worship Hall) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 (Main Shrine, Important Cultural Property) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Chokushaden.jpg|直会殿 (Banquet Hall) </gallery>
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * '''Main Shrine''' (附 棟札6枚) - Built in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the late Muromachi period. Designated on August 28, 1907.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|102|2676|都祁水分神社本殿}}</ref>
=== Nara Prefectural Designation === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''' ** '''Gilded Bronze Mikoshi''' (Portable Shrine) - Constructed in 1456 (Kōshō 3). Designated on March 31, 1975.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Official Website).</ref> == See Also == * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}} * {{ill|Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuge-mikumari-jinja}} * [http://www.tsugemikumari.or.jp Official Website of Tsuge Mikumari Shrine] * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=20501 Tsuge Mikumari Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program: Shinto and Shrine Historical Resources Collection

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Tsuge-mikumari-jinja]][[ja:都祁水分神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Tsuge-mikumari-jinja]][[ja:都祁水分神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tsuge-mikumari-jinja]][[ja:都祁水分神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Shaden.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.59722222222222|N|135.94722222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q22119448]] Wikidata name: Tsuge-mikumari-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tsuge Mikumari Shrine''' (都祁水分神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (major shrine) and formerly held the Rank of a [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Prefectural shrine]] under the old shrine Ranking system.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following three deities: * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|qq=}} * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|lt=Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|天水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}} (Heavenly Water Distribution Deity) * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|lt=Kuni-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|国水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}} (Earthly Water Distribution Deity)
== History ==
=== Early History === The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is traditionally believed to have been established during the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. Initially located at the site of {{ill|Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine|ja|都祁山口神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine|qq=}}, it was later moved to its current location, believed to have been used as the Horigoe Temporary Palace during the reign of Emperor Shōmu. The area served as a lodging site for the imperial princesses who were priestesses of the Ise Grand Shrine during the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]].
The shrine was listed as a major shrine in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] and received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-no-Matsuri]].
=== Medieval Period === During the medieval period, the shrine's lands became part of the estate of [[:en:Kōfuku-ji|Kōfuku-ji]], specifically under the Kita-in sub-temple of the Nikaidō lineage. As the estate developed, the worship of water deities grew in importance. In 972 (Tenroku 3), the shrine was relocated to its current site in Tomoda. The current main shrine building was constructed in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the Muromachi period.
=== Divine Ranks === * [[:en:Jōwa|Shōwa (era)]] 7 (840), October 7: Elevated from unranked to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower ([[:en:Shoku Nihon Kōki|Shoku Nihongi]]) * {{ill|Tian’an|ja|天安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tian’an|1=Cheonan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3577288}} 3 (859), January 27: Elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower ([[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]])
== Grounds == <gallery> File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Maiden.jpg|舞殿 (Dance Hall) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Haiden.jpg|拝殿 (Worship Hall) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 (Main Shrine, Important Cultural Property) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Chokushaden.jpg|直会殿 (Banquet Hall) </gallery>
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * '''Main Shrine''' (附 棟札6枚) - Built in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the late Muromachi period. Designated on August 28, 1907.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|102|2676|都祁水分神社本殿}}</ref>
=== Nara Prefectural Designation === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''' ** '''Gilded Bronze Mikoshi''' (Portable Shrine) - Constructed in 1456 (Kōshō 3). Designated on March 31, 1975.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Official Website).</ref> == See Also == * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}} * {{ill|Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuge-mikumari-jinja}} * [http://www.tsugemikumari.or.jp Official Website of Tsuge Mikumari Shrine] * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=20501 Tsuge Mikumari Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program: Shinto and Shrine Historical Resources Collection

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tsuge-mikumari-jinja]][[ja:都祁水分神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tsuge-mikumari-jinja]][[ja:都祁水分神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tsuge-mikumari-jinja]][[ja:都祁水分神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Shaden.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.59722222222222|N|135.94722222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q22119448]] Wikidata name: Tsuge-mikumari-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tsuge Mikumari Shrine''' (都祁水分神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (major shrine) and formerly held the Rank of a [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Prefectural shrine]] under the old shrine Ranking system.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following three deities: * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|qq=}} * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|lt=Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|天水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}} (Heavenly Water Distribution Deity) * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|lt=Kuni-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|国水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}} (Earthly Water Distribution Deity)
== History ==
=== Early History === The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is traditionally believed to have been established during the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. Initially located at the site of {{ill|Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine|ja|都祁山口神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine|qq=}}, it was later moved to its current location, believed to have been used as the Horigoe Temporary Palace during the reign of Emperor Shōmu. The area served as a lodging site for the imperial princesses who were priestesses of the Ise Grand Shrine during the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]].
The shrine was listed as a major shrine in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] and received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-no-Matsuri]].
=== Medieval Period === During the medieval period, the shrine's lands became part of the estate of [[:en:Kōfuku-ji|Kōfuku-ji]], specifically under the Kita-in sub-temple of the Nikaidō lineage. As the estate developed, the worship of water deities grew in importance. In 972 (Tenroku 3), the shrine was relocated to its current site in Tomoda. The current main shrine building was constructed in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the Muromachi period.
=== Divine Ranks === * [[:en:Jōwa|Shōwa (era)]] 7 (840), October 7: Elevated from unranked to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower ([[:en:Shoku Nihon Kōki|Shoku Nihongi]]) * {{ill|Tian’an|ja|天安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tian’an|1=Cheonan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3577288}} 3 (859), January 27: Elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower ([[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]])
== Grounds == <gallery> File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Maiden.jpg|舞殿 (Dance Hall) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Haiden.jpg|拝殿 (Worship Hall) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 (Main Shrine, Important Cultural Property) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Chokushaden.jpg|直会殿 (Banquet Hall) </gallery>
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * '''Main Shrine''' (附 棟札6枚) - Built in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the late Muromachi period. Designated on August 28, 1907.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|102|2676|都祁水分神社本殿}}</ref>
=== Nara Prefectural Designation === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''' ** '''Gilded Bronze Mikoshi''' (Portable Shrine) - Constructed in 1456 (Kōshō 3). Designated on March 31, 1975.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Official Website).</ref> == See Also == * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}} * {{ill|Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuge-mikumari-jinja}} * [http://www.tsugemikumari.or.jp Official Website of Tsuge Mikumari Shrine] * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=20501 Tsuge Mikumari Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program: Shinto and Shrine Historical Resources Collection

revision.comment: Adding categories based on Wikidata P31: Junior Fifth Rank, Senior Fifth Rank, Shikinai Taisha, Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings, Kanpei-sha
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tsuge-mikumari-jinja]][[ja:都祁水分神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tsuge-mikumari-jinja]][[ja:都祁水分神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tsuge-mikumari-jinja]][[ja:都祁水分神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Shaden.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.59722222222222|N|135.94722222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q22119448]] Wikidata name: Tsuge-mikumari-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tsuge Mikumari Shrine''' (都祁水分神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara City}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (major shrine) and formerly held the Rank of a [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Prefectural shrine]] under the old shrine Ranking system.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following three deities: * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|qq=}} * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|lt=Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|天水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}} (Heavenly Water Distribution Deity) * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|lt=Kuni-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|国水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}} (Earthly Water Distribution Deity)
== History ==
=== Early History === The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is traditionally believed to have been established during the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}}. Initially located at the site of {{ill|Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine|ja|都祁山口神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine|qq=}}, it was later moved to its current location, believed to have been used as the Horigoe Temporary Palace during the reign of Emperor Shōmu. The area served as a lodging site for the imperial princesses who were priestesses of the Ise Grand Shrine during the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}.
The shrine was listed as a major shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}} and received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}.
=== Medieval Period === During the medieval period, the shrine's lands became part of the estate of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|en|Kōfuku-ji|WD=Q1070863|lt=Kōfuku-ji}}, specifically under the Kita-in sub-temple of the Nikaidō lineage. As the estate developed, the worship of water deities grew in importance. In 972 (Tenroku 3), the shrine was relocated to its current site in Tomoda. The current main shrine building was constructed in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the Muromachi period.
=== Divine Ranks === * {{ill|Jōwa|en|Jōwa|WD=Q3577279|lt=Shōwa (era)}} 7 (840), October 7: Elevated from unranked to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower ({{ill|Shoku Nihon Kōki|en|Shoku Nihon Kōki|WD=Q1041301|lt=Shoku Nihongi}}) * {{ill|Tian’an|ja|天安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tian’an|1=Cheonan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3577288}} 3 (859), January 27: Elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}})
== Grounds == <gallery> File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Maiden.jpg|舞殿 (Dance Hall) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Haiden.jpg|拝殿 (Worship Hall) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 (Main Shrine, Important Cultural Property) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Chokushaden.jpg|直会殿 (Banquet Hall) </gallery>
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * '''Main Shrine''' (附 棟札6枚) - Built in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the late Muromachi period. Designated on August 28, 1907.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|102|2676|都祁水分神社本殿}}</ref>
=== Nara Prefectural Designation === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''' ** '''Gilded Bronze Mikoshi''' (Portable Shrine) - Constructed in 1456 (Kōshō 3). Designated on March 31, 1975.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Official Website).</ref> == See Also == * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}} * {{ill|Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuge-mikumari-jinja}} * [http://www.tsugemikumari.or.jp Official Website of Tsuge Mikumari Shrine] * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=20501 Tsuge Mikumari Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program: Shinto and Shrine Historical Resources Collection

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tsuge-mikumari-jinja]][[ja:都祁水分神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tsuge-mikumari-jinja]][[ja:都祁水分神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tsuge-mikumari-jinja]][[ja:都祁水分神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Shaden.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.59722222222222|N|135.94722222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} '''Tsuge Mikumari Shrine''' (都祁水分神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara City}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (major shrine) and formerly held the Rank of a [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Prefectural shrine]] under the old shrine Ranking system.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following three deities: * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|qq=}} * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|lt=Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|天水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}} (Heavenly Water Distribution Deity) * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|lt=Kuni-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|国水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}} (Earthly Water Distribution Deity)
== History ==
=== Early History === The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is traditionally believed to have been established during the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}}. Initially located at the site of {{ill|Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine|ja|都祁山口神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine|qq=}}, it was later moved to its current location, believed to have been used as the Horigoe Temporary Palace during the reign of Emperor Shōmu. The area served as a lodging site for the imperial princesses who were priestesses of the Ise Grand Shrine during the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}.
The shrine was listed as a major shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}} and received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}.
=== Medieval Period === During the medieval period, the shrine's lands became part of the estate of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|en|Kōfuku-ji|WD=Q1070863|lt=Kōfuku-ji}}, specifically under the Kita-in sub-temple of the Nikaidō lineage. As the estate developed, the worship of water deities grew in importance. In 972 (Tenroku 3), the shrine was relocated to its current site in Tomoda. The current main shrine building was constructed in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the Muromachi period.
=== Divine Ranks === * {{ill|Jōwa|en|Jōwa|WD=Q3577279|lt=Shōwa (era)}} 7 (840), October 7: Elevated from unranked to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower ({{ill|Shoku Nihon Kōki|en|Shoku Nihon Kōki|WD=Q1041301|lt=Shoku Nihongi}}) * {{ill|Tian’an|ja|天安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tian’an|1=Cheonan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3577288}} 3 (859), January 27: Elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}})
== Grounds == <gallery> File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Maiden.jpg|舞殿 (Dance Hall) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Haiden.jpg|拝殿 (Worship Hall) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 (Main Shrine, Important Cultural Property) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Chokushaden.jpg|直会殿 (Banquet Hall) </gallery>
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * '''Main Shrine''' (附 棟札6枚) - Built in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the late Muromachi period. Designated on August 28, 1907.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|102|2676|都祁水分神社本殿}}</ref>
=== Nara Prefectural Designation === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''' ** '''Gilded Bronze Mikoshi''' (Portable Shrine) - Constructed in 1456 (Kōshō 3). Designated on March 31, 1975.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Official Website).</ref> == See Also == * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}} * {{ill|Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuge-mikumari-jinja}} * [http://www.tsugemikumari.or.jp Official Website of Tsuge Mikumari Shrine] * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=20501 Tsuge Mikumari Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program: Shinto and Shrine Historical Resources Collection

<!--Tsuge Mikumari Shrine--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Tsuge-mikumari-jinja}}
{{wikidata link|Q22119448}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Nara City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:pages]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tsuge-mikumari-jinja]][[ja:都祁水分神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tsuge-mikumari-jinja]][[ja:都祁水分神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Tsuge-mikumari-jinja]][[ja:都祁水分神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Shaden.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.59722222222222|N|135.94722222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} '''Tsuge Mikumari Shrine''' (都祁水分神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara City|1=Nara (city)}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (major shrine) and formerly held the Rank of a [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Prefectural shrine]] under the old shrine Ranking system.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following three deities: * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|qq=}} * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|lt=Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|天水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}} (Heavenly Water Distribution Deity) * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|lt=Kuni-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|国水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}} (Earthly Water Distribution Deity)
== History ==
=== Early History === The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is traditionally believed to have been established during the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}}. Initially located at the site of {{ill|Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine|ja|都祁山口神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine|qq=}}, it was later moved to its current location, believed to have been used as the Horigoe Temporary Palace during the reign of Emperor Shōmu. The area served as a lodging site for the imperial princesses who were priestesses of the Ise Grand Shrine during the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}.
The shrine was listed as a major shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}} and received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niiname-no-Matsuri|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}.
=== Medieval Period === During the medieval period, the shrine's lands became part of the estate of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|en|Kōfuku-ji|WD=Q1070863|lt=Kōfuku-ji|1=Kōfuku-ji}}, specifically under the Kita-in sub-temple of the Nikaidō lineage. As the estate developed, the worship of water deities grew in importance. In 972 (Tenroku 3), the shrine was relocated to its current site in Tomoda. The current main shrine building was constructed in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the Muromachi period.
=== Divine Ranks === * {{ill|Jōwa|en|Jōwa|WD=Q3577279|lt=Shōwa (era)|1=Jōwa}} 7 (840), October 7: Elevated from unranked to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower ({{ill|Shoku Nihon Kōki|en|Shoku Nihon Kōki|WD=Q1041301|lt=Shoku Nihongi|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}}) * {{ill|Tian’an|ja|天安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tian’an|1=Cheonan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3577288}} 3 (859), January 27: Elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}})
== Grounds == <gallery> File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Maiden.jpg|舞殿 (Dance Hall) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Haiden.jpg|拝殿 (Worship Hall) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 (Main Shrine, Important Cultural Property) File:Tsuge Mikumari Jinja Chokushaden.jpg|直会殿 (Banquet Hall) </gallery>
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * '''Main Shrine''' (附 棟札6枚) - Built in 1499 (Meiō 8) during the late Muromachi period. Designated on August 28, 1907.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|102|2676|都祁水分神社本殿}}</ref>
=== Nara Prefectural Designation === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''' ** '''Gilded Bronze Mikoshi''' (Portable Shrine) - Constructed in 1456 (Kōshō 3). Designated on March 31, 1975.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Official Website).</ref> == See Also == * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}} * {{ill|Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuge-mikumari-jinja}} * [http://www.tsugemikumari.or.jp Official Website of Tsuge Mikumari Shrine] * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=20501 Tsuge Mikumari Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program: Shinto and Shrine Historical Resources Collection
